hero.subtitle: A Claude Code native framework powered by 29 mvt-* skills. Zero-copy CLI install — hand your full software lifecycle to a virtual team of Analyst, Architect, Developer, Reviewer, and Tester.

install.title: Start Building in Seconds
install.description: One CLI, zero copy. Run npx @uoyo/mvtt install inside any project — pick a language, pick an architecture pattern, and /mvt-init inside Claude Code.

features.subtitle: Nine core capabilities that make your AI-powered development team efficient, modular, and Claude Code native.

items.title: Role Separation
items.desc: Say goodbye to chaotic single-LLM outputs. Each agent has a precise role and focuses on what it does best.

items.title: Claude Code Native
items.desc: Built on Claude Code skills. 29 /mvt-* slash commands drop straight into your editor — no scaffolding to clone.

items.title: Unified Registry
items.desc: A manifest-driven install/update/uninstall/doctor lifecycle with hash integrity checks — upgrade safely in place.

items.title: Context Contract
items.desc: Each agent declares its prerequisites clearly to eliminate hallucinations and false inferences.

items.title: Workspace Architecture
items.desc: Session, project-context, artifacts, knowledge, and templates live under .ai-agents/ — portable and git-friendly.

items.title: Semantic Index
items.desc: Project-specific guidelines and domain knowledge are semantically indexed and loaded on demand.

items.title: Semi-automatic workflows
items.desc: Not completely blind automation. Developers guide design approval before code generation starts.

items.title: Extensible Skills
items.desc: Use /mvt-create-skill to spin up team-specific slash commands. Skills are reusable, shareable, git-committable.

items.title: Multilingual Output
items.desc: Pick preferences.language at install time — artifacts, reviews, and architecture docs come out in your team's language.

workflow.title: Seamless Development Lifecycle
workflow.subtitle: How /mvt-* slash commands drive the pipeline.

items.desc1: Extract business concepts, analyze PRD documents.
items.desc2: Structured requirement breakdown and acceptance criteria.

items.desc1: Establish tech stack, plan system/module architecture.
items.desc2: Verifiable architectural blueprints and DB/API design.

items.desc1: Implement business code according to the blueprint.
items.desc2: Business domain code strictly adhering to context contracts.

items.desc1: Deep code dive, execute static and standard checks.
items.desc2: Refactoring suggestions and quality improvement reports.

items.desc1: Design test cases for core business paths.

stories.title: Real Teams, Real Workflows
stories.subtitle: Three ways developers put MVTT to work.

items.title: Inheriting a legacy codebase
items.scenario: Handed an undocumented Java monolith. Two weeks to map out the domain model.

items.title: Starting a new project from scratch
items.scenario: Building a SaaS subscription system from a PM-authored PRD.

items.title: Customizing test generation
items.scenario: Team enforces strict Gherkin/internal templates — default /mvt-test doesn't quite fit.

items.name: Conductor
items.desc: The core dispatcher managing task flow. Initializes the team and hands work to the right agent.

items.name: Analyst
items.desc: Transforms long-form documents or messy code into solid business domain concepts.

items.name: Architect
items.desc: Based on analysis, establishes the tech stack and designs strict architectural blueprints.

items.name: Developer
items.desc: Flawlessly follows the blueprint to write elegant, production-grade code.

items.name: Reviewer
items.desc: Critiques submitted code with extremely strict standards to find flaws.

items.name: Tester
items.desc: Explores edge cases and builds automated testing scripts.

commands.title: Master Your Commands
commands.subtitle: After npx @uoyo/mvtt install, use these /mvt-* slash commands inside Claude Code.

cmds.desc: Initialize the virtual team and project structure.
Check current workflow and pipeline status.
List all available skills and usage examples.
Clean up chat history and stale artifacts.

cta.titlePart1: Ready to hire your AI Tech Team?
cta.subtitle: Install once, type /mvt-init in Claude Code, and your virtual team is on the job.
